title: Update working memory from popup visual-effect selector session
summary: Durable context from the session that replaced the snow-only popup toggle with a visual-effect selector and iterated on sakura and rain effects.

claims.text: Visual effects use `gvVisualEffect` as one sync setting with current values `off`, `snow`, `sakura`, and `rain`; the popup writes that key and clears legacy `gvSnowEffect`, while effect loaders still resolve legacy `gvSnowEffect: true` to snow for backward compatibility.

On `gemini.google.com`, content initialization starts the snow, sakura, and rain effect modules together; each module decides whether to render from the shared `gvVisualEffect` value instead of the content entrypoint branching by effect.

The visual-effect modules render independent full-screen `pointer-events:none` canvas overlays, pause animation on hidden tabs, clean up on `beforeunload`, and use a draining state so particles finish falling before the canvas is removed when disabled or switched away.

The popup visual-effect control should be an elegant compact icon selector, not a boolean snow on/off switch; the user asked for a sliding-bar style control that switches among off, snow, and sakura, and the accepted implementation extended it to rain as a fourth option.

The session rejected sakura implementations based on flattened circles/ellipses and full `scaleX = cos(...)` flipping because the user judged them perfunctory or leaf-like rather than like cherry blossoms.

The accepted sakura direction is a pale somei-yoshino style: wide rounded petals with a shallow top notch and bottom stem point, slow airy drift, and gentle positive wobble instead of aggressive 3D tumbling.

The rain visual effect uses `gvVisualEffect: 'rain'` and renders cool blue-grey, wind-angled line streaks across three depth layers, with splash ripples for mid and near drops when they hit the viewport floor.

The `StorageKeys.GV_VISUAL_EFFECT` inline comment still documents the value set as `off | snow | sakura`, even though the popup and runtime now also support `rain`; future edits should update that comment so it does not mislead maintainers.

metadata.reason: The opening user request asked to replace the snow enable switch with a sliding-bar style selector using elegant concise icons; the final assistant summary reports the accepted popup as four choices including rain.
The user challenged the initial sakura result as perfunctory and later said it felt more like falling leaves; the assistant summaries identify the rejected flattened-circle and full-flip shapes.
After the user's leaf-like feedback, the accepted summary describes the final sakura changes as rounder, paler, slower, and gently wobbling without full flips.
